$schema_notes: Golden parity matrix for L6E-101 — embedding wire-shape contract.
Canonical path: l6e/tests/fixtures/embedding_validation_parity.json
- l6e/tests/unit/test_embedding_parity.py (runs l6e.cloud._validate_embedding)
- hosted-edge/tests/test_embedding_parity.py (runs POST /v1/authorize; reads this file from sibling l6e/ in monorepo)
Both sides must agree on every case. If you tighten or loosen one side,
regenerate this file and run BOTH suites before merging.
Encoding (kept strict-JSON-portable so jq / tooling work):
String element sentinels (substituted at test load time):
"__nan__"     → float('nan')
"__inf__"     → float('inf')
"__neg_inf__" → float('-inf')
Inflated inputs (avoid 4096-element JSON arrays inline):
{"__repeat__": {"value": V, "count": N}}
→ expanded to a list of N copies of V before the test consumes it.
Case schema:
name             : kebab-case test ID, must be unique
input            : the value passed to _validate_embedding / sent on the wire
expected         : 'valid' | 'invalid'
expected_coerced : optional list of floats; when present, the SDK validator
must return exactly this list. Mainly for int→float coercion cases.
notes            : optional human-readable rationale
Server-side wire shape: send {... base body ..., "request_embedding": <input>}.
expected='valid'   → POST returns 200
expected='invalid' → POST returns 400
Null input is intentionally NOT covered — the absence sentinel is a separate
concern from the shape contract; both sides treat null as 'no embedding'.

cases.name: rejects_above_dim_cap_4097
cases.notes: Boundary: one over the cap must reject.

cases.name: rejects_nan_element
cases.notes: NaN-in would propagate poisonously through the Layer 2 predictor (L6E-68). Catch at the wire.

cases.name: rejects_bool_true_element
cases.notes: bool is an int subclass; silent True→1 promotion would mask wire bugs.

cases.name: rejects_int_input
cases.notes: Bare scalar — must be a list.
